data.title: Short-term JGB yields dip as weak data tempers rate hike bets
data.description:
TOKYO, Feb 16 (Reuters) - Short-term Japanese government bond (JGBs) yields edged lower on Monday as weaker-than-expected economic data caused traders to ‚Äãpare bets for an early rate hike by the ‚Äåcentral bank.

The two-year yield, which is most sensitive to Bank of Japan (BOJ) policy rates, fell 1.5 basis points (bps) ‚ÅÝto 1.265%. The five-year yield fell 1.5 bps to 1.665%. Bond yields move inversely to ‚ÅÝprices.

Swaps markets trimmed bets for a BOJ interest rate ‚Äåhike in April after ‚Äådata showed Japan's gross domestic product edged up an annualised 0.2% in the October-December quarter, short of the median ‚Äãestimate for a 1.6% gain.

Later in the ‚Äåday, BOJ Governor Kazuo Ueda will meet with Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi for their first bilateral meeting since the ruling party's landslide election ‚Äãvictory earlier this month.

"We expect the government ‚Äãto leave ‚Äåmonetary policy decisions largely up to the BOJ, but will pay close attention to communications from both the government and the BOJ following the Takaichi-Ueda meeting," Naohiko ‚ÅÝBaba, chief Japan economist at Barclays in Tokyo, wrote in a note.

Barclays ‚ÅÝstill expects the BOJ to raise rates at its April meeting, he added.

The benchmark 10-year yield was flat at 2.210% after a three-day drop.

The 20-year yield climbed 3 bps to 3.075%. The 30-year yield added 4.5 bps to 3.075%, while the yield on ‚ÅÝthe 40-year ‚ÄåJGB rose 7 bps to 3.725%.

Long-term JGB yields surged to ‚Äårecord highs last month on concerns about the potential scale of stimulus from Prime Minister ‚ÅÝTakaichi, a fiscal dove.

But a measure of calm has returned to the market following her party's sweeping election victory on expectations that the mandate will give her the leeway to keep to her pledge of "responsible" stimulus.

"It seems like the government of Japan is least cognizant of markets and what their impact might be," said Tom Garretson, senior portfolio strategist at RBC ‚ÄãWealth Management.

"If they don't push too far on fiscal measures and eroding the balance sheet further, it looks like there's at least a balance between what the markets are ‚Äãwilling to tolerate and the goals ‚Äåof the administration." (Reporting by Rocky Swift in Tokyo; Editing ‚Äãby Sumana Nandy and Thomas Derpinghaus)

data.title: ROI-Suffocating Western pressure may finally force Russian oil output cuts: Bousso
data.description:
(The opinions expressed here are those of the author, a columnist for Reuters)

By Ron Bousso

LONDON, Feb 16 (Reuters) - Russian oil producers could be forced to sharply cut output in coming months as tightening pressure from U.S. President Donald Trump and European powers restricts the country's exports and its storage fills up, a development that would further dent the Kremlin's war chest.

Russian crude exports have remained broadly stable in recent years despite sweeping Western sanctions and a sharp reduction in energy purchases by Europe. Moscow successfully redirected most of its seaborne crude to China, ‚ÄãIndia and Turkey, often relying on a "shadow fleet" of ageing, uninsured tankers to circumvent restrictions while offering steep discounts.

That ‚Äåresilience is now under strain. Exports have slowed in recent months after President Trump tightened sanctions and imposed tariffs on India over its purchases of Russian oil.

DROP IN SEABORNE EXPORTS

Demand has also been hit by a European Union ban on imports of fuels refined ‚ÅÝfrom Russian crude that came into force last month.

Russian seaborne crude exports fell to 3.4 million barrels per day (bpd) in January from 3.8 million bpd in December, and are currently tracking ‚ÅÝaround 2.8 million bpd in February, according to analytics firm Kpler.

At the same time, the volume of Russian oil held on ships has ‚Äåclimbed to a record high above 150 million ‚Äåbarrels in recent months, while many tankers have also slowed their speeds - both signs of weaker buying.

The European Commission's proposal to impose a sweeping prohibition on any business that supports Russia's seaborne crude oil exports - which goes far beyond previous sanctions - would squeeze Moscow ‚Äãyet further.

INDIA SLASHES IMPORTS

Pressure on Russian exports is likely to intensify in the coming months as India, ‚Äåthe largest buyer of seaborne Russian oil last year, prepares to curb purchases as part of a trade deal with the United States. President Trump has said New Delhi agreed to halt Russian imports under the deal, though Indian officials have not confirmed that plan.

India bought around 1.7 million bpd of Russian ‚Äãcrude last year, roughly half of Moscow's total seaborne exports, according to Kpler. Imports fell to about ‚Äã1.1 million bpd in ‚ÄåJanuary and, while they are expected to rebound slightly in February, they are also projected to decline sharply from March onward.

Three major refiners - Indian Oil, Bharat Petroleum and Reliance Industries - have halted purchases of Russian crude, Reuters has reported.

Indian buying is unlikely to drop to zero, however. Nayara Energy's 400,000 bpd refinery, partly owned by Russian oil ‚ÅÝmajor Rosneft, will continue to rely on Russian supplies, though it is scheduled to shut for maintenance for one month starting in April.

Smaller Indian refiners are also likely to keep ‚ÅÝbuying Russian crude, particularly if discounts deepen.

Independent Chinese refineries may also absorb some of the displaced volumes, but their scope is limited. Russia already accounted for about one-fifth of China's total crude imports of 11.5 million bpd last year, and Beijing has historically avoided overreliance on any single supplier.

SUPPLY CHAIN REACTION

This slowdown in purchases is triggering a negative chain reaction across Russia's oil logistics. Longer "shadow fleet" voyages are tying up tankers, reducing the availability of vessels to store additional crude at sea, which, in turn, is forcing producers to divert more oil into domestic storage.

The size of Russia's onshore storage capacity is unclear, ‚ÅÝas the government does not ‚Äåpublish data, but the amount remaining appears limited. Onshore oil inventories stand at around 16 million barrels, or about 51% of capacity, ‚Äåbased on satellite monitoring of tanks, according to Kpler senior crude analyst Naveen Das.

Russia could use parts of its vast pipeline network for storage if needed. Kpler estimates that pipeline storage and fixed-top tanks, whose storage ‚ÅÝlevels cannot be monitored by satellite, could raise total onshore capacity to around 100 million barrels.

But even that buffer may prove insufficient. Russia produces about 9.3 million bpd of crude, roughly half of which is exported. At that rate, onshore storage could fill quickly if exports remain constrained, leaving producers with few options other than cutting output.

BUDGETS ARE UNDER STRAIN

Russia's oil production could fall by up to 300,000 bpd between March and May as a result of these logistical bottlenecks, according to Jorge Leon, head of geopolitical analysis at Rystad Energy.

Oil and gas revenues are the Kremlin's main source of income, accounting for nearly a quarter of federal budget receipts. Moscow's finances have already been strained by heavy defence spending since Russia launched the full-scale invasion of Ukraine in 2022.

The country's state oil and gas revenues halved in January from a year earlier, falling to their lowest level since ‚ÄãJuly 2020, as declining crude prices took their toll, according to finance ministry data.

A drop in production combined with deeper discounts on exports would further erode Moscow's oil income, compounding the financial pressure on the Russian state - which is precisely what the West is seeking to do as the deadly conflict in Ukraine enters its fourth year.

Enjoying this column? Check out Reuters Open Interest (ROI), your essential new source ‚Äãfor global financial commentary. Follow ROI on LinkedIn, and X. Listen to the Morning Bid ‚Äådaily podcast on Apple, Spotify, or the Reuters app. Subscribe to hear Reuters journalists discuss the biggest news in markets and finance seven ‚Äãdays a week.

(Reporting by Ron Bousso; Editing by David Holmes)

data.title: Volkswagen plans to cut costs by 20% by end 2028, Manager Magazin reports
data.description:
Feb 16 (Reuters) - Volkswagen plans to cut costs by 20% across all brands by ‚Äãthe end of 2028, ‚ÄåManager Magazin reported on Monday.

According to ‚ÅÝinformation obtained by the magazine, ‚ÅÝCEO Oliver Blume and ‚Äåfinance chief ‚ÄåArno Antlitz have presented a "massive" savings ‚Äãplan at a ‚Äåclosed-door meeting with the company's top executives in ‚ÄãBerlin in mid-January.

The ‚Äãgroup's ‚Äåcost-cutting initiative is aimed at ensuring returns go back to ‚ÅÝa sustainable level amid a ‚ÅÝslump in China, U.S. tariffs and a competitive environment, the magazine said.

Where exactly the ‚ÅÝsavings ‚Äåare to be made ‚Äåand where cooperation between ‚ÅÝthe brands is to be improved remained unclear at the meeting, the magazine said, but plant closures could also be on the ‚Äãtable.

VW did not immediately reply to an emailed request for ‚Äãcomment.

(Writing by ‚ÄåLinda Pasquini, editing by ‚ÄãKirsti Knolle)

data.description:
News Highlights:

  • Enterprises across India will gain access to a new 200MW deployment of the AMD ‚ÄúHelios‚Äù rack-scale AI architecture, supporting India‚Äôs AI initiatives and sovereign AI factories.¬Ý¬Ý
  • AMD and TCS will help enterprise customers accelerate AI at scale with an AI training and inference platform designed to improve operational efficiency, time-to-deployment, and real-world enterprise impact across industries.

SANTA CLARA, Calif. and MUMBAI, India, Feb. 16, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- AMD , a leader in high-performance and AI computing, and Tata Consultancy Services (TCS) (BSE: 532540, NSE: TCS), a global leader in IT services, consulting, and business solutions, have expanded their strategic collaboration. TCS, through its subsidiary HyperVault AI Data Center Limited (HyperVault), and AMD will codevelop a rack-scale AI infrastructure design based on the AMD ‚ÄúHelios‚Äù platform in support of India‚Äôs national AI initiatives.

Powered by AMD Instinct‚Ñ¢ MI455X GPUs, next-generation AMD EPYC‚Ñ¢ ‚ÄúVenice‚Äù CPUs, AMD Pensando‚Ñ¢ Vulcano NICs and the open ROCm‚Ñ¢ software ecosystem, ‚ÄúHelios‚Äù is purpose-built to deliver a rack-scale AI platform supporting sovereign AI factories. ‚ÄúHelios,‚Äù combined with TCS‚Äô enterprise expertise and scale, will accelerate deployment and enhance operational efficiencies for enterprises. As part of this strategic collaboration, both companies will offer an AI-ready data center blueprint supporting up to 200 MW of capacity and will work with hyperscalers and AI companies to accelerate data center build-outs in India.

Dr. Lisa Su, Chair and CEO, AMD, said, "AI adoption is accelerating from pilots to large-scale deployments, and that shift requires a new blueprint for compute infrastructure. With 'Helios,' we are delivering an open, rack-scale AI platform designed for performance, efficiency, and long-term flexibility. Together with TCS, we are enabling enterprises across India to deploy AI at scale today while building the compute foundation of tomorrow.‚Äù

K. Krithivasan, MD and CEO, TCS, said, ‚ÄúThis collaboration lays the foundation for AMD‚Äôs first ‚ÄòHelios‚Äô powered AI infrastructure in India. By combining our strengths in AI, connectivity, sustainable power, and advanced data center engineering, we are poised to deliver state-of-the-art infrastructure solutions for AI companies and global enterprises. We are thrilled to deepen our longstanding partnership with AMD as we expand our participation in the AI ecosystem ‚Äì Infrastructure to Intelligence.‚Äù

TCS established HyperVault in 2025 with the vision of delivering GW-scale, secure, and reliable AI-ready infrastructure for hyperscalers, AI companies, and global enterprises. This announcement builds on the recent strategic collaboration between TCS and AMD to help enterprises scale AI adoption and modernize hybrid environments.

data.title: Research Alert: CFRA Maintains Hold Opinion On Shares Of Rollins Inc.
data.description:
01:10 AM EST, 02/16/2026 (MT Newswires) -- CFRA, an independent research provider, has provided MT Newswires with the following research alert. Analysts at CFRA have summarized their opinion as follows:

Following ROL's Q4 print, we keep our $60 target, at 42.5x our 2027 EPS estimate, in line with ROL's five-year forward average but higher than its Environmental & Facilities Services peers. We keep our 2026 EPS view at $1.26 for 2026 and set 2027's at $1.42. ROL's Q4 was mixed, with sales and EPS performance falling short of expectations. Revenue growth of 9.7% Y/Y consisted of 5.7% in organic growth and 4.0% from acquisitions, with the seasonal side of the pest business (~20% of sales) impacted by erratic weather, according to management. The termite segment led growth once again, at +7.6% organic Y/Y, while residential services +9.7% overall but only +4.4% organically. Beyond top-line headwinds, ROL faced margin compression as adj. operating margin fell 30 bps Y/Y to 18.3%, indicating that cost pressuresparticularly employee costsremain an ongoing concern. Management was optimistic on cost savings looking to 2026, though we remain cautious given that margin headwinds are lasting longer than anticipated.

MT Newswires does not provide investment advice. Unauthorized reproduction is strictly prohibited.

data.title: PRECIOUS-Gold drops over 1% as thin trading, profit-taking weigh
data.description:
(Updates prices for Asia mid-session trade)

*

Gold falls over 1%; silver down over 3%

*

China markets closed February 15-23 for Lunar New Year holiday

*

US markets closed on Monday for Presidents' Day holiday

By Pablo Sinha

Feb 16 (Reuters) -

Gold prices dropped on ‚ÄãMonday, pressured by thin trading volumes as U.S. and ‚ÄåChina markets remained shut due to local public holidays, while some traders booked profits after last session's 2.5% jump. Spot ‚ÅÝgold fell 1.1% to $4,986.32 per ounce by 0550 GMT. U.S. gold futures for ‚ÅÝApril delivery lost 0.8% to $5,005.60 per ounce. "Gold has given ‚Äåback some of ‚ÄåFriday's post-CPI gains today due to thinner trading conditions and a lack of fresh upside catalysts," said Tim Waterer, ‚ÄãKCM chief analyst, referring to the U.S. ‚Äåconsumer price inflation data. He also pointed to profit-taking on the day.

U.S. markets are closed for the Presidents' Day holiday, while markets in ‚ÄãChina are closed for the Lunar ‚ÄãNew Year holiday. The ‚ÄåU.S. CPI rose 0.2% in January after an unrevised 0.3% gain in December, the Labor Department's Bureau of Labor Statistics said on Friday. Economists polled ‚ÅÝby Reuters had forecast the CPI to increase by 0.3%. Federal Reserve Bank ‚ÅÝof Chicago President Austan Goolsbee said on Friday that interest rates could go down, but noted that services inflation remained high. Market participants anticipate the central bank to hold interest rates at its next meeting on March 18. Still, they are pricing in ‚ÅÝ75 ‚Äåbasis points in rate cuts this year, with the first expected ‚Äåin July, according to data compiled by LSEG.

Non-yielding bullion tends to do well ‚ÅÝin low-interest-rate environments. "It will likely require the dollar to resume its downtrend for gold to make a push in the direction of $6,000 before year-end," Waterer said. On the geopolitical front, the U.S. military is preparing for the possibility of a weeks-long operation against Iran should President Donald Trump authorise an attack, two U.S. officials told Reuters, in what could become a far more ‚Äãserious conflict than previously seen between the countries. Spot silver lost 2.4% to $75.64 per ounce, after a 3% fall earlier in the session. The white metal rose 3.4% ‚Äãon Friday. Spot platinum slipped 0.8% to $2,045.11 per ‚Äåounce, while palladium shed 0.7% to $1,673.52. (Reporting by Pablo Sinha in ‚ÄãBengaluru; Editing by Harikrishnan Nair)

data.title: Research Alert: CFRA Keeps Sell Opinion On Shares Of Tc Energy Corporation
data.description:
12:30 AM EST, 02/16/2026 (MT Newswires) -- CFRA, an independent research provider, has provided MT Newswires with the following research alert. Analysts at CFRA have summarized their opinion as follows:

Our 12-month target price of CAD66, raised by CAD3, reflects a combination of our relative valuation and DCF models. On a relative basis, we apply a 12x multiple of enterprise value to projected 2027 EBITDA, in line with TRP's historical forward average, and yielding a value of CAD72 per share. Meanwhile, our DCF model, based on a free cash flow growth rate of 9.3% per year, terminal value of 2.5%, and a WACC of 5.9%, yields a value of CAD60 per share. We lift our 2026 EPS estimate by CAD0.01 to CAD3.81, and start 2027's at CAD3.90. We see TRP as somewhat of a step-down in capital spending intensity, with an expected CAD4.0 billion of projects going into service in 2026, less than half of the CAD8.3 billion that went into service in 2025. TRP has been executing quite well, coming in on-time and under-budget, in 2025. Nonetheless, we estimate that shares are trading almost 15% above TRP's historical forward average, which we find tough to justify. Shares yield 4.1%.

MT Newswires does not provide investment advice. Unauthorized reproduction is strictly prohibited.

data.title: ByteDance pledges to prevent unauthorised IP use on AI video tool after Disney threat
data.description:
Feb 15 (Reuters) -

ByteDance will take steps to prevent the unauthorised use of intellectual property on its AI video generator Seedance 2.0, the Chinese technology firm said on Monday, following threats ‚Äãof legal action from U.S. studios, including Disney ( DIS ).

Videos ‚Äågenerated by Seedance 2.0, which was released last week, have gone viral in China including ‚ÅÝone of Tom Cruise and Brad Pitt in a fight. The AI ‚ÅÝmodel has been compared to DeepSeek and has been ‚Äåpraised for its ‚Äåability to produce cinematic storylines with just a few prompts. Disney ( DIS ) sent a cease-and-desist letter to ‚ÄãByteDance accusing the Chinese firm of using ‚ÄåDisney characters to train and power Seedance 2.0 without permission, a source familiar with the matter told Reuters late on ‚ÄãSunday.

Disney ( DIS ) said ByteDance had pre-packaged ‚ÄãSeedance with ‚Äåa pirated library of copyrighted characters from franchises including Star Wars and Marvel, portraying them as if they were public-domain clip art, the ‚ÅÝperson said. The letter alleged Seedance was reproducing, distributing and creating derivative ‚ÅÝworks featuring Spider-Man, Darth Vader, and other characters, the person added.

"We are taking steps to strengthen current safeguards as we work to prevent the unauthorized use of intellectual property and likeness by users," ByteDance said ‚ÅÝin ‚Äåits statement.

It did not elaborate on the measures it ‚Äåwas taking.

Online news outlet Axios was the first to report on ‚ÅÝDisney's move. Paramount Skydance ( PSKY ) has also sent a cease-and-desist letter to ByteDance, accusing the Chinese firm of engaging in "blatant infringement" of its intellectual property, Variety reported at the weekend.

Disney ( DIS ) has taken similar actions against Character.AI, demanding that the startup immediately stop the unauthorised use of its copyrighted characters. In December, Disney ( DIS ) signed a licensing deal ‚Äãwith OpenAI, letting the startup use characters from Star Wars, Pixar and Marvel franchises in its Sora video generator. (Reporting by Rajveer Singh Pardesi in Bengaluru; ‚ÄãAdditional reporting Clare Jim in ‚ÄåHongkong and Eduardo Baptista in Beijing; Editing by Miyoung ‚ÄãKim and Kate Mayberry)

data.title: Australia's BlueScope Steel beats profit estimates, lifts outlook; shares fall
data.description:
Feb 16 (Reuters) - Australian takeover target BlueScope Steel ( BLSFF ) forecast on Monday higher second-half earnings and reported first-half profit above estimates, helped by stronger U.S. spreads, higher volumes across key markets, and good cost control.

The steelmaker expects ‚Äãsecond-half underlying earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT) to ‚Äåbe between A$620 million and A$700 million ($437.72 million and $494.20 million).

For the six months ended December 31, BlueScope posted underlying ‚ÅÝEBIT of A$557.5 million and underlying net profit after tax (NPAT) of A$382 million, more ‚ÅÝthan double last year's A$176.4 million and above a Visible ‚ÄåAlpha estimate of A$349.2 ‚Äåmillion.

Despite the upbeat result, the stock ended the session down 2.7% at A$28.37, after swinging to a drop ‚Äãof 3.3% from a gain of 3.2%.

"Today's ‚Äåmove looks less like a verdict on the quality of the result and more like a recalibration around second-half expectations and where we sit ‚Äãin the cycle," said Marc Jocum, ‚Äãsenior product and ‚Äåinvestment strategist at Global X ETFs.

After last month's rejection of a A$13.2 billion takeover proposal from SGH and U.S.-based Steel Dynamics ( STLD ), parts of the market ‚ÅÝhad priced in hopes of a higher rival bid in the range of ‚ÅÝA$35 to A$40 versus the original A$30 offer.

With no fresh corporate developments, focus has shifted back to fundamentals and near-term earnings risk, Jocum added.

The group said it was strongly positioned to generate more cash flows, for a total of A$3 a share to ‚ÅÝshareholders ‚Äåin 2026, including a special dividend of A$1 a share announced ‚Äåearlier.

It declared an interim dividend of 65 Australian cents a share, up from 30 ‚ÅÝcents a year earlier, and announced a A$310 million on-market buyback, in line with its distribution target to return at least 75% of free cash flow to shareholders.

"In the half, all major projects have progressed towards completion, including the EAF at New Zealand Steel ..., all of which underpin BlueScope's operational resilience and growth," said Managing Director and CEO Tania Archibald.

The company has also ‚Äãsimplified its portfolio with the sale of 50% stake in Tata BlueScope Steel and sold a portion of its West Dapto site, setting the stage to increase shareholder returns, ‚ÄãArchibald said.

($1=1.4164 Australian dollars)

(Reporting by Sneha ‚ÄåKumar and Roshan Thomas in Bengaluru; Editing by Alistair Bell, ‚ÄãDiane Craft and Clarence Fernandez)

data.title: Treasury Wine pauses dividend after large writedown on US business
data.description:
By Roushni Nair and Byron Kaye

Feb 16 (Reuters) - Australia's Treasury Wine Estates ( TSRYF ) on Monday suspended its interim dividend after booking a writedown on its U.S. business that resulted in a huge net loss.

Shares in the company, one of the world's top five winemakers by volume and owner of ‚Äãthe Penfolds label, finished down 4.4% after sliding as much as ‚Äå7% at one point.

The company has been hammered by falling demand for wine in China as well as in the U.S., with consumers ‚ÅÝshifting away from alcohol and grappling with rises in the cost of living. Treasury has also ‚ÅÝhad to deal with distribution problems in California, a key market. The ‚Äåstock more than halved in ‚Äåvalue in 2025.

On a statutory basis, Treasury swung to a A$649.4 million ($460 million) loss for the half-year ended December after logging a ‚ÄãA$770.5 million impairment on its U.S. assets - a somewhat ‚Äålarger writedown than what had been flagged late last year. The result marks its first half-year loss since the company split from the Foster's group in 2011.

But even ‚Äãhigher up on its profit and loss statement, ‚Äãthe company was ‚Äåclearly in pain. Operating income tumbled 40% to A$236.4 million on a 17% plunge in revenue to A$1.3 billion.

LOTS OF WORK TO DO IN CHINA

Seeking to tackle its problems, Treasury ‚ÅÝannounced in December it was looking to cut annual costs by A$100 million over the next ‚ÅÝtwo to three years by trimming channel stock which would steady pricing and rebuild brand momentum.

That plan is progressing well, the company said in a statement.

It said suspending its interim dividend would help preserve capital and bring debt back within its target range - a move analysts at Jefferies called "sensible". It paid a ‚ÅÝdividend of 20 ‚ÄåAustralian cents per share for the same period a year ago.

Asked when ‚Äåthe company would resume paying dividends, Chief Financial Officer Stuart Boxer said the company was focused on paying ‚ÅÝdown debt and lifting cashflow, "but it is too early to call, at this stage, the timing of those".

"Ultimately, it will be a decision for the board," he said on a call with analysts.

It has also tightened shipments into China to curb parallel imports - where Treasury's premium wines like Penfolds are imported into the country by other companies and are generally sold for far less than those by authorised dealers.

While Treasury had flagged much of the earnings ‚Äãpain in December, Citi analysts noted that depletions - volumes of wine sold to end-users - in China fell 17.2% from August to December, adding that the company "has a lot of work to do" to reignite demand.

($1 = 1.4114 ‚ÄãAustralian dollars)

(Reporting by Roushni Nair in Bengaluru and ‚ÄåByron Kaye in Sydney; Additional reporting by Shivangi Lahiri and Sneha Kumar ‚Äãin Bengaluru; Editing by Edwina Gibbs)

data.title: Qube shares hit record high after agreeing to $8.3 billion Macquarie-led buyout
data.description:
Feb 16 (Reuters) - Shares of Australia's Qube Holdings shot to a record high on Monday after it agreed to an A$11.7 billion ($8.26 billion) buyout offer from a consortium led by Macquarie Asset Management (MAM) ‚Äãto take the logistics firm private.

Qube ( QUBHF ) shares jumped ‚Äåas much as 4.1% in early trading to an all-time high of A$5.05. The stock remained below the ‚ÅÝconsortium's A$5.20 a share offer.

The agreement follows months of talks after a MAM-led ‚ÅÝconsortium made a non-binding offer in November for Qube ( QUBHF ), which ‚Äågave the company an ‚Äåenterprise value of A$11.6 billion. Qube ( QUBHF ) owns ports, intermodal terminals and bulk handling facilities across Australia.

Qube's ( QUBHF ) board ‚Äãrecommended its investors vote in favour of ‚Äåthe deal at a shareholder meeting that is due to be held later this year. The transaction requires Australian competition and foreign ‚Äãinvestment regulatory approval.

"Qube ( QUBHF ) is a really great ‚Äãreflection of ‚Äåthe Australian economy, if you think what is driving the Australian economy, a lot of the volume is similar to what Qube's ( QUBHF ) volume is," said ‚ÅÝAni Satchcroft, MAM's co-head of infrastructure for Asia-Pacific.

"It's imports such as containers ‚ÅÝand vehicles, and then export demand across things like resources and agriculture, for us that macroeconomic reflection was a really big attraction of Qube ( QUBHF )."

Australian pension fund UniSuper, Qube's ( QUBHF ) largest investor with a 15.1% stake, and Pontegadea, the family office of Spain's ‚ÅÝOrtega ‚Äåfamily, which is known for controlling global clothing company Inditex, ‚Äåjoined the Macquarie consortium. Fast fashion retailer Zara is an Inditex subsidiary.

UniSuper began increasing ‚ÅÝits stake by buying Qube ( QUBHF ) shares on the open market after Macquarie's bid was first unveiled in November.

"There will be more public-to-private transactions," John Pearce, UniSuper's chief investment officer, told Reuters.

"There's a feeling that in a private vehicle, there is more of an opportunity to actually take longer-term investment decisions."

Under a scheme implementation deed, the Sydney-based firm can pay dividends ‚Äãof up to a maximum of 40 Australian cents, which will result in a reduction in the offer price, both Qube ( QUBHF ) and MAM said.

($1 = 1.4172 Australian dollars)

(Reporting by ‚ÄãScott Murdoch in Sydney, Sameer Manekar ‚Äåand John Biju in Bengaluru; Editing by Vijay Kishore, ‚ÄãDiane Craft and Sonali Paul)

data.title: Walmart, economic data await investors confronting AI 'whack-a-mole'
data.description:
NEW YORK, Feb 13 (Reuters) - U.S. stock investors will be on guard next week for further volatility induced by fears of artificial intelligence disruption as they also assess the durability of a rotation beneath the market's surface, along with upcoming earnings from Walmart ( WMT ) and fresh economic data.

The benchmark S&P 500 closed on Thursday down 0.2% for the year, but that modest change belies significant swings in ‚Äãpockets of the market.

After sinking shares of software companies this month, fears that ‚Äånew AI tools will disrupt various industries, including insurance, wealth management and transportation, slammed stocks this week.

"It's all this whack-a-mole game of trying to figure out what AI is going to destroy next ‚ÅÝin a world where you can invent a narrative, because this technology is so new that artificial intelligence is likely going to ‚ÅÝend up eating the whole world," said Art Hogan, chief market strategist at B Riley Wealth. "That's probably ‚Äånot the case, but that's where ‚Äåwe are right now in that sentiment."

The swoons for various industries to start 2026 contrast with much of last year, when optimism over AI-driven profits and capital spending helped drive a ‚Äãbroad swath of stocks higher.

AI winner and loser moves in single stocks "are getting ‚Äåmore and more extreme," Jonathan Krinsky, BTIG's chief market technician, said in a note on Thursday morning.

"At a certain point ... we begin getting concerned that the weakness supersedes the strength and the broad market becomes vulnerable," Krinsky said.

ROTATIONS PAVING ‚ÄãWAY FOR NEW MARKET LEADERS?

Pressure from AI has also contributed to ‚Äãdeclines for the heavyweight ‚Äåtechnology sector, which has mostly led the gains for the bull market that began in October 2022, but was last down over 4% this year.

Broadening gains have helped offset tech's troubles, with investors moving into groups that have lagged. Four sectors are ‚ÅÝup at least 10% in 2026 - energy, consumer staples, materials and industrials - while small-cap stocks have also posted outsized increases.

"We're starting ‚ÅÝto get an embedded leadership shift that's undeniable at this point," said Mark Hackett, chief market strategist at Nationwide. "This shift is now getting embedded into the psychology of investors."¬Ý

Tech retains a major presence in U.S. indexes, including a one-third weighting in the S&P 500. Even if tech weakness drags down the market barometers, investors have said wider participation in equity gains bodes well for the market's health.

"It's been really difficult to make those new ‚ÅÝall-time highs because ‚Äåof the absence of tech leadership," said¬ÝKevin Gordon, head of macro research and strategy at Charles Schwab. "But ‚Äåthis is not necessarily a bad thing."

WALMART EARNINGS, INFLATION DATA ON TAP

Walmart's ( WMT ) quarterly results headline the batch of corporate earnings reports due in the coming ‚ÅÝweek as the fourth-quarter reporting season winds down.

The retailing bellwether offers Wall Street a view into consumer spending trends after data this week showed U.S. retail sales were unexpectedly unchanged in December. Other retailers will follow with their reports over the next few weeks, including Home Depot, Lowe's and Target.

With its stock up 20% this year, Walmart ( WMT ) recently pushed its market capitalization above $1 trillion. It is by far the biggest company by market value in the consumer staples sector, which is up 15% in 2026.

U.S. traders face a shortened week due to a holiday on Monday. Economic reports include the advance reading of fourth-quarter GDP, a monthly consumer ‚Äãsentiment survey, and the personal consumption expenditures price index, a key inflation measure.

Data this week showed a surprising jump in U.S. job growth in January, suggesting signs of labor market stability.

Some of the sectors that have been part of the "catch-up trade" in recent weeks are also sensitive to the ‚Äãhealth of the economy, Gordon said.

"To some extent, that is ‚Äåpricing in maybe not a firm re-acceleration in the economy, but I think at least a stabilization," ‚ÄãGordon said.¬Ý¬Ý

(Reporting by Lewis KrauskopfEditing by Rod Nickel)

data.title: GRAPHIC-Europe's earnings gain pace while lofty valuations cap rewards
data.description:
LONDON, Feb 16 (Reuters) - European company earnings growth is picking up this reporting season against a tentatively improving economic backdrop, but wary investors are demanding more than solid results to justify sky-high valuations. Companies representing 57% of Europe's market capitalisation have reported so far, achieving average earnings growth of 3.9% in the fourth quarter, ahead of estimates for a final result of a contraction of 1.1% based on LSEG I/B/E/S data.

"Overall, ‚ÄãEPS recovery is on track, especially in Europe," said Barclays European equity strategist Magesh Kumar Chandrasekaran.

Here ‚Äåare the top themes emerging from the earnings season:

SKIP A BEAT

So far, 60% of European companies have beaten earnings expectations, compared to a typical quarter when 54% would beat analyst estimates, according to ‚ÅÝLSEG I/B/E/S. Companies that have outperformed expectations, however, have not been as likely to be rewarded with a positive share price reaction, analysts said. Deutsche Bank noted ‚ÅÝthat the net price reaction of a stock on the day of earnings has been flat for ‚Äåcompanies that beat expectations and negative in ‚Äålow-single-digit percentages for misses.

"It is to do with the higher valuations we're at," said Carolin Raab, European equity and cross-asset strategist at Deutsche Bank.

"At those valuations, it's typical to see some short-term pullbacks and ‚Äãa bit more nervousness around earnings, even though what we are hearing from companies ‚Äåis not so bad." Europe's STOXX 600 currently trades on a multiple of 15.3 times forward earnings, its highest since January 2022.

STRONGER EURO FACTORED IN

The STOXX 600 is an international index and generates almost 60% of its revenue outside Europe. So ‚Äãthe persistent strength of the euro, which rose above $1.20 for the first ‚Äãtime in over four years ‚Äålast month, remains a key consideration for the region's companies.

Dorian Carrell, head of multi-asset income at Schroders, said the strength of the single currency is something that companies have been factoring in.

"The large part of it is done, in the sense that U.S. companies have ‚ÅÝbenefitted and it's been a headwind for Europe," Carrell said.

"From here, the trajectory of the dollar and euro from our perspective is less ‚ÅÝclear, which would probably be an advantage to European companies."

TARIFF UNCERTAINTY RECEDES AND REALITY KICKS IN

Analysis from market intelligence platform AlphaSense showed the number of companies mentioning tariffs in earnings calls dropped sharply since the middle of last year, when U.S. President Donald Trump's trade plans rattled markets, but that doesn't mean that there have been no ramifications for European companies.

"We're definitely starting to see the impact of tariffs feeding through," said Sutanya Chedda, European equity strategist at UBS.

"Some companies ‚ÅÝare passing it ‚Äåon to consumers, while some are taking a hit on margins."

BANKS STILL LEAD, SET TO BE AI WINNERS

There ‚Äåare only a handful of sectors that have posted earnings growth in Q4, with financials being one of them.

Deutsche Bank's Raab said this quarter is ‚ÅÝthe twelfth in a row that banks have, on net, beaten estimates.

"We're seeing financials as the sector with the most guidance upgrades relative to downgrades," Raab said.

"We still like the sector and the earnings environment still looks pretty good." While losers from artificial intelligence have dominated the news agenda recently, UBS believes the banking sector will be "net winners", even though AI has made little difference to near-term earnings estimates.

TECH DISPERSION Nothing has highlighted the dispersion in technology stocks quite as much as the recent performance from the euro zone's largest company, Dutch ASML, and its fourth-largest, Germany's SAP. ASML, whose tools are used by chipmakers including the world's largest, Taiwan's TSMC, raised its sales outlook due to ‚Äãsurging demand from the AI buildout. In contrast, software company SAP, which was Europe's largest company as recently as March last year, tumbled 16% on earnings day as concerns grew about AI's disruptive effects on the software sector.

"Semiconductors have done so much better than software companies recently," Schroders' Carrell said.

"Our philosophy is to ‚Äãlook where expectations and valuations are low, so we think that might ‚Äåbe a little bit overdone," Carrell added, noting that software valuations are now cheaper than for hardware.

(Reporting by ‚ÄãSamuel Indyk; Editing by Amanda Cooper and Elaine Hardcastle)

data.title: Research Alert: CFRA Maintains Hold Opinion On Shares Of Cleveland-cliffs Inc.
data.description:
02:05 PM EST, 02/13/2026 (MT Newswires) -- CFRA, an independent research provider, has provided MT Newswires with the following research alert. Analysts at CFRA have summarized their opinion as follows:

We decrease our 12-month target price by $3 to $12, assuming an EV/EBITDA of 8.0x our 2027 EBITDA estimate, a small discount to its three-year average forward EV/EBITDA of 8.4x and peer average of 8.3x. We lower our EPS estimate in 2026 from $0.69 to a loss per share of $0.39, and we initiate our 2027 EPS forecast at $0.81. CLF's outlook hinges on three key catalysts: automotive reshoring, the expiration of its value-destructive slab contract, and improved Canadian market dynamics. Management expects shipments of 16.5-17.0 million tons in 2026, up from 16.2 million in 2025, driven by multiyear fixed-price automotive contracts and higher utilization rates. The termination of the ArcelorMittal slab contract should add approximately $500 million in EBITDA as CLF redirects capacity to higher-margin flat-rolled products. With 50% Section 232 tariffs supporting domestic pricing and melted-import requirements driving demand, CLF is well positioned to benefit without requiring major capital investments.

MT Newswires does not provide investment advice. Unauthorized reproduction is strictly prohibited.

data.title: METALS-LME copper, aluminium slip in thin China holiday trade
data.description:
Feb 16 (Reuters) - London copper eased on Monday, while aluminium extended losses for a third straight session as thin trade, with China out for a holiday, weighed on prices.

The ‚ÄãShanghai Futures Exchange is closed for ‚Äåthe Lunar New Year holiday that began on February 15 and trading will resume on ‚ÅÝFebruary 24.

The benchmark three-month copper on the London Metal Exchange, fell ‚ÅÝ0.5% to $12,810.0 a ton as of 0449 ‚ÄåGMT, having dropped ‚Äåto a one-week low on Friday.

LME copper may retest a support at $12,578 a ‚Äãmetric ton this week, a break ‚Äåbelow which could trigger a drop into $11,545-$12,116 range, Reuters technical analyst Wang Tao said on Monday.

Copper stocks ‚Äãon the world's three biggest ‚Äãmetal exchanges ‚Äåhave exceeded 1 million metric tons for the first time in more than two decades, as an inventory build ‚ÅÝdue to soft demand in China adds to recent stockpiling ‚ÅÝin the United States.

LME copper stocks were up 7,225 metric tonnes by Friday.

China's factory activity faltered in January as weak domestic demand dragged down production at the start of the ‚ÅÝnew year, ‚Äåan official survey showed on January 31.

In ‚Äåother metals, aluminium nudged 0.4% lower to $3,065.0, having dropped to ‚ÅÝa one-week low on Friday after a report that U.S. President Donald Trump plans to scale back some tariffs on steel and aluminum goods.

LME aluminium may retest a support at $3,000 a metric ton this week, a break below could open the way toward $2,916, Tao added.

LME nickel lost ‚Äã0.5% to $16,905/ton, its lowest in more than a week. Nickel stocks on the exchange were also up 702 tons.

Zinc fell 0.5% ‚Äãand lead dipped 0.10%. (Reporting by ‚ÄåIshaan Arora and Swati Verma in Bengaluru; Editing ‚Äãby Sumana Nandy)

data.title: Trump Tariffs Backfired, 130,000 Jobs Added In January And More: This Week In Economy
data.description:
It’s the start of a new week, and we’re here to provide a quick round-up of the top stories from the last week. Here’s what you need to know:

Trump Admits ‘Mistake’ In Passing Over Kevin Warsh For Fed Chair In 2017

President Donald Trump has admitted to making a significant error in not appointing Kevin Warsh as the Federal Reserve Chair in 2017. Trump acknowledged this as a “really big mistake” and attributed it to the influence of his former Treasury Secretary. This revelation was made during an interview with Fox Business.

Trump revealed that Warsh was his initial choice for the position, but then-Treasury Secretary¬ÝSteven Mnuchin swayed him.¬ÝWarsh¬Ýwas the runner-up in the selection process that ultimately led to Jerome Powell becoming the central bank’s leader.

Read the full article here.

Goldman Sachs Highlights 7 Stocks To Benefit From Memory Shortage

Goldman Sachs ( GS ) has identified seven stocks that could benefit from the ongoing global memory shortage. The shortage, particularly in memory chips like DRAM, NAND, and HBM, is predicted to drive earnings and margins for companies in this sector to cycle highs.

Giuni Lee, an analyst at Goldman Sachs ( GS ), stated that the supply/demand landscape for memory chips is currently one of the tightest in over a decade.

Read the full article here.

Trump Tariffs Backfired: 94% Of Economic Burden Fell On US Importers, NY Fed Says

A report released by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York revealed that American businesses and consumers, rather than foreign exporters, bore nearly the entire financial burden of the 2025 tariffs. Despite a significant increase in the average U.S. tariff rate from 2.6% to 13%, foreign prices did not drop significantly to offset the hike.

Read the full article here.

US Economy Adds 130,000 Jobs In January

The U.S. economy added 130,000 jobs in January, according to data released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics. However, this figure was overshadowed by a major revision that saw over a million jobs vanish. The initial report had indicated a gain of 467,000 jobs in January, but this was revised to a loss of 533,000 jobs.

Read the full article here.

China Directs Banks to Cut US Treasury Holdings

China has reportedly instructed its banks to reduce their purchases of U.S. Treasury bonds, potentially dampening foreign demand for U.S. debt. Economist Peter Schiff cautioned that if the Federal Reserve intervenes to buy the bonds, it could lead to higher consumer prices.

Read the full article here.

data.title: US Could Soon Enter A 'Debt Spiral', Exceeding Post-World War II Debt-To-GDP Record By 2030, Watchdog Says
data.description:
The U.S. is approaching a critical fiscal point as its federal debt is projected to surpass historical levels, raising alarms about the country’s economic stability.

What Is The CBO Saying?

The U.S. federal debt is on track to surpass a significant milestone, with projections indicating a troubling fiscal future. The Congressional Budget Office (CBO) reports that publicly held debt stands at $31 trillion, equating to roughly 100% of the nation’s GDP.

By 2030, the debt is expected to exceed the post-World War II record of 106% and could reach 120% by 2036. This increase is driven by rising annual interest costs, which are projected to more than double to $2.1 trillion by 2036, further straining federal budgets and increasing deficits.

‚ÄòDebt Spiral'

"CBO's latest baseline shows an unsustainable fiscal outlook, with debt approaching record levels, deficits remaining elevated at more than twice a reasonable target, and interest costs exploding," the Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget said in a note on Wednesday.

"Later in the decade, under CBO's baseline, the average interest rate on all federal debt will exceed nominal economic growth, which could represent the start of a debt spiral."

The Treasury Department is experiencing higher bond yields, influenced by previous Federal Reserve rate hikes and concerns over the U.S.’s global financial reliability. The average interest rate on federal debt is currently 3.316% and is expected to rise to 3.9% by 2036.

The Committee for a Responsible Federal Budget warns of an unsustainable fiscal path, with debt potentially reaching 131% of GDP by 2036 if certain tariffs are deemed illegal. This scenario could lead to a debt spiral and a heightened risk of fiscal crisis.

Rising US Debt A Growing Concern

The rising U.S. debt has been a growing concern among economists and policymakers. Richard Haass, president of the Council on Foreign Relations, has warned that the $38 trillion national debt poses a significant threat to the country’s security and global standing, potentially leading to a "national security crisis."

Additionally, the impact of President Donald Trump’s tariffs has been a double-edged sword. While they have driven the S&P 500 and Dow to record highs, they also risk increasing household costs by $1,300 annually.

Despite these challenges, the U.S. economy showed signs of strength, with the Atlanta Fed’s GDPNow model predicting a strong 5.4% GDP growth in Q4 2025, marking the strongest quarterly growth since 1984.

data.title: Disney sends cease-and-desist to ByteDance over AI-generated videos
data.description:
Feb 15 (Reuters) - Disney ( DIS ) has sent a cease-and-desist letter to ByteDance accusing the Chinese company of using Disney ( DIS ) characters to train and power its Seedance 2.0 AI video generator without ‚Äãpermission, a source familiar with the ‚Äåmatter told Reuters.

Disney ( DIS ) said ByteDance had pre-packaged Seedance with a pirated library of copyrighted ‚ÅÝcharacters from franchises including Star Wars and Marvel, portraying them ‚ÅÝas if they were public-domain clip art, the ‚Äåperson said.

The letter ‚Äåalleges Seedance is reproducing, distributing and creating derivative works featuring Spider-Man, Darth Vader, ‚Äãand other characters, the person added.

ByteDance ‚Äåwill strengthen safeguards on its Seedance 2.0 AI video tool to prevent the unauthorised use of copyrighted ‚Äãcharacters and celebrity likenesses, the ‚ÄãChinese firm ‚Äåtold the BBC on Sunday.

ByteDance did not immediately respond to Reuters' request for comment.

Online news outlet Axios was the first ‚ÅÝto report on Disney's ( DIS ) move. Paramount Skydance has also ‚ÅÝsent a cease-and-desist letter to ByteDance, accusing the Chinese firm of engaging in "blatant infringement" of its intellectual property, Variety reported at the weekend.

Videos generated by Seedance 2.0, which was released last ‚ÅÝweek, have ‚Äågone viral in China including one of Tom ‚ÄåCruise and Brad Pitt in a fight. The AI model ‚ÅÝhas been compared to DeepSeek and has been praised for its ability to produce cinematic storylines with just a few prompts.¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý

Disney ( DIS ) has taken similar actions against Character.AI, demanding that the startup immediately stop the unauthorised use of its copyrighted characters.

In December, Disney ( DIS ) signed a licensing deal with ‚ÄãOpenAI, letting the startup use characters from Star Wars, Pixar and Marvel franchises in its Sora video generator.

(Reporting by Rajveer Singh ‚ÄãPardesi in Bengaluru; Additional reporting ‚ÄåClare Jim in Hongkong; Editing by Miyoung ‚ÄãKim and Kate Mayberry)

Nikkei, Wall St futures shade firmer in thin trade

*

Japan GDP grows just 0.2% in Q4, far below forecast

*

Dollar idles ahead of US GDP, global PMIs

By Wayne Cole

SYDNEY, Feb 16 (Reuters) - Asian shares quietly consolidated recent hefty gains on Monday as the Lunar New Year holiday made for thin trading, while dismal economic data out of Japan took some air out of that booming market. China, South Korea, Taiwan were among the markets ‚Äãthat were closed, leaving currencies and bonds becalmed, but precious metals under fresh pressure.

Japan reported its ‚Äåeconomy grew a miserly 0.2% annualised in the December quarter, far below the 1.6% gain forecast as government spending dragged on activity.

The disappointing figures underline the tough task ahead for Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi and should support ‚ÅÝher push for more aggressive fiscal stimulus.

Japan's Nikkei edged up 0.2%, having climbed 5% last week. MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan firmed 0.4%.

South Korea's ‚ÅÝtech-heavy market surged 8.2% last week, while Taiwan climbed almost 6% for the week.

"Our fear in Asia is that ‚Äåif the mega-cap technology companies announce a pause ‚Äåin capital expenditure, that might lead to a sharp correction in memory stocks that have rallied sharply in markets like Korea this year," said Nick Ferres, chief investment officer at Vantage Point.

"While rotation is likely ‚Äãto favour emerging markets, we are becoming increasingly cautious on memory stocks in Korea and Taiwan ‚Äåfollowing their exceptional performance and re-rating."

For Europe, EUROSTOXX 50 futures rose 0.1%, while DAX futures and FTSE futures added 0.2%.

S&P 500 futures gained 0.2%, while Nasdaq futures rose 0.1%.

The major data of the week are not out until Friday when surveys of global manufacturing hit ‚Äãand the U.S. reports gross domestic product for the fourth quarter.

Median forecasts are ‚Äãfor annualised growth of ‚Äå3.0%, down from 4.4% the previous quarter but still solid.

MORE CAPEX MEANS FEWER BUYBACKS Earnings season continues in the U.S., with the star attraction being Walmart ( WMT ), which will provide a gauge on consumer spending trends after a disappointing December for retail sales.

The retailer's stock has jumped 20% this ‚ÅÝyear, taking its market capitalisation above $1 trillion and making it by far the biggest company by market value in the consumer staples sector , which is ‚ÅÝup 15% in 2026.

Defensive stocks have benefited from a rotation out of tech amid concerns about the huge cost of AI capex and the disruptive effect of AI competition on sectors such as software, which has shed 24% in market value in the past three months.

Hyperscaler capex plans have ballooned to $660 billion, $120 billion higher than at the start of the earnings season. Analysts at Goldman Sachs noted that as capex has surged, S&P 500 buybacks have dropped by 7% from a year ago.

"This marks the ‚ÅÝthird consecutive quarter ‚Äåof stagnation," they wrote in a note. "We expect the increasing scarcity of free cash flows and buybacks will strengthen ‚Äåthe premium for companies focused on returning cash flows to shareholders." There is no lack of cash flowing into bond markets as money exited stocks and U.S. economic data underpinned ‚ÅÝthe case for more rate cuts from the Federal Reserve.

Yields on two-year Treasuries fell to 3.408% on Friday, the lowest close since mid-2022. Futures imply a 68% chance the Fed will cut in June and have 62 basis points of easing priced in for the year.

The drop in yields pulled the dollar index down 0.8% last week to 96.890, with most of the losses against a rebounding Japanese yen.

The dollar was 0.2% firmer on Monday at 153.05 yen , having sunk 2.9% last week, while the euro was flat at $1.1866.

The dollar also shed 1% on the Swiss franc last week, while the euro slid under 0.9100 francs for the first time since 2015. The relentless rise of the franc has markets on alert for possible ‚Äãintervention from the Swiss National Bank given inflation is already at 0.1%, close to the bottom of its 0% to 2% target band.

In commodity markets, gold slid 1.3% to $4,973 an ounce , having swung wildly in recent weeks as some investors were squeezed out of leveraged positions. Silver lost 3% to $75.05 an ounce. Oil prices were steady as ‚Äãinvestors digested a Reuters report that OPEC is leaning towards a resumption ‚Äåof oil output increases from April.

Brent was flat at $67.77 a barrel, while U.S. crude barely budged at $62.91 per barrel. (Reporting ‚Äãby Wayne Cole; Editing by Sonali Paul and Kate Mayberry)

data.title: Wild New Zealand storm disrupts transport, leaves thousands without power
data.description:
*

Air New Zealand ( ANZFF ) pauses operations at Wellington airport

*

Storm is forecast to move toward South Island

*

Several schools closed, 30,000 properties without power

(Adds latest update on flights, weather; paragraphs 2-3,10)

By Renju Jose

SYDNEY, Feb 16 (Reuters) - Heavy rain and strong winds disrupted flights, trains ‚Äãand ferries, forcing the closure of roads across large ‚Äåparts of New Zealand's North Island on Monday, while snapping power links to tens of thousands.

Domestic media reported a few ‚ÅÝflights had resumed operating by afternoon from the airport in Wellington, the capital, although cancellations were ‚ÅÝstill widespread after airport authorities said most morning flights were disrupted.

Air ‚ÄåNew Zealand said it ‚Äåhoped to resume services when conditions ease later on Monday, after it paused operations at Wellington, Napier and Palmerston North ‚Äãairports.

Online images showed flooded semi-rural neighbourhoods, inundated homes, ‚Äåtrees fallen on vehicles and collapsed sections of road after waters receded.

The weather had been "absolutely terrifying", Marilyn Bulford, who lives in the rural town of Bunnythorpe, ‚Äãabout 160 km (100 miles) north of Wellington, ‚Äãtold the New ‚ÄåZealand Herald newspaper.

"I've never seen huge trees blowing around this much," she added. "It's so bad. I haven't seen anything like it."

The Wellington region accounted for more than ‚ÅÝhalf the 852 emergency calls received overnight, said Ken Cooper, assistant national commander of the ‚ÅÝemergency services.

"We had a very busy night, and our firefighters are continuing to respond to calls," he added.

More than 30,000 properties were without power, including about 10,000 customers in Wellington, said authorities, who have urged motorists to stay off roads, while several schools were closed as emergency ‚ÅÝcrews ‚Äåtackled widespread damage.

The storm is forecast to bring heavy rains as it ‚Äåheads for the east coast of the South Island on Tuesday, the weather bureau said, with ‚ÅÝauthorities warning of further disruption.

Raw sewage discharged after this month's failure of Wellington's main wastewater treatment plant in a storm was washed back onto the south coast by the weekend storm, in an incident some residents called a "poonami" on social media.

A low-pressure system east of the North Island has battered several regions since the weekend, bringing heavy rain and severe gales. A man was found dead on Saturday ‚Äãin a submerged vehicle on a highway.

The storm follows six deaths last month in a landslide triggered by heavy rains at Mount Maunganui on the North Island's east coast, bringing down soil ‚Äãand rubble on a site crowded with ‚Äåfamilies on summer holidays. (Reporting by Renju Jose in Sydney; Editing ‚Äãby Stephen Coates and Clarence Fernandez)

data.title: Disney sends cease-and-desist to ByteDance over AI-generated videos
data.description:
Feb 15 (Reuters) - Disney ( DIS ) has sent a cease-and-desist letter to ByteDance accusing the Chinese company of using Disney ( DIS ) characters to train and power its Seedance 2.0 AI video generator without ‚Äãpermission, a source familiar with the ‚Äåmatter told Reuters.

Disney ( DIS ) said ByteDance had pre-packaged Seedance with a pirated library of copyrighted ‚ÅÝcharacters from franchises including Star Wars and Marvel, portraying them ‚ÅÝas if they were public-domain clip art, the ‚Äåperson said.

The letter ‚Äåalleges Seedance is reproducing, distributing and creating derivative works featuring Spider-Man, Darth Vader, ‚Äãand other characters, the person added.

ByteDance ‚Äåwill strengthen safeguards on its Seedance 2.0 AI video tool to prevent the unauthorised use of copyrighted ‚Äãcharacters and celebrity likenesses, the ‚ÄãChinese firm ‚Äåtold the BBC on Sunday.

ByteDance did not immediately respond to Reuters' request for comment.

Online news outlet Axios was the first ‚ÅÝto report on Disney's ( DIS ) move. Paramount Skydance has also ‚ÅÝsent a cease-and-desist letter to ByteDance, accusing the Chinese firm of engaging in "blatant infringement" of its intellectual property, Variety reported at the weekend.

Videos generated by Seedance 2.0, which was released last ‚ÅÝweek, have ‚Äågone viral in China including one of Tom ‚ÄåCruise and Brad Pitt in a fight. The AI model ‚ÅÝhas been compared to DeepSeek and has been praised for its ability to produce cinematic storylines with just a few prompts.¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý¬Ý

Disney ( DIS ) has taken similar actions against Character.AI, demanding that the startup immediately stop the unauthorised use of its copyrighted characters.

In December, Disney ( DIS ) signed a licensing deal with ‚ÄãOpenAI, letting the startup use characters from Star Wars, Pixar and Marvel franchises in its Sora video generator.

(Reporting by Rajveer Singh ‚ÄãPardesi in Bengaluru; Additional reporting ‚ÄåClare Jim in Hongkong; Editing by Miyoung ‚ÄãKim and Kate Mayberry)

data.title: New Zealand's a2 Milk lifts FY revenue growth forecast; shares hit near 5-year high
data.description:
Feb 16 (Reuters) - New Zealand's a2 Milk ( ACOPF ) raised its full-year revenue growth forecast on Monday after half-year profit beat expectations on strong sales momentum in ‚Äãits top market, China, sending its shares ‚Äåto a near five-year high.

The dairy producer said it now expects fiscal 2026 revenue to ‚ÅÝrise by a mid-double-digit percentage from last year's continuing operations, up ‚ÅÝfrom its prior forecast of low double-digit growth.

It ‚Äåalso expects fiscal ‚Äå2026 net profit after tax to increase from last year's reported level, compared with ‚Äãits earlier guidance that profit would ‚Äåbe only "slightly up" year on year.

Shares jumped as much as 11.7% to NZ$11.17, their highest level since February 24, ‚Äã2021, before closing up 5%.

For ‚Äãthe half-year ‚Äåended December 31, the company's net profit after tax attributable from continuing operations increased 9.4% to NZ$112.1 million ($67.66 million), topping a ‚ÅÝVisible Alpha consensus estimate of NZ$104 million.

Revenue from the firm's ‚ÅÝkey China-and-other-Asia segment jumped 20.3% during the period, helped by growth in its English-label infant milk formula and other nutritionals.

Segment sales rose to NZ$739 million, above the Visible Alpha estimate of NZ$714 million, ‚ÅÝdriven ‚Äåby 6.5% growth in China-label infant milk formula revenue ‚Äåand a 23.9% increase in English-label sales.

The company has stepped up ‚ÅÝmarketing investment, particularly in China, while strengthening support for domestic growth and supply chain initiatives.

"Market share gains are driving this growth along with new product launches - any slowdown to share gains or new product traction presents a risk," said Matt Montgomerie, senior equities analyst at Forsyth Barr.

A2 Milk ( ACOPF ) declared ‚Äãan interim dividend of 11.5¬ÝNew Zealand cents per share, up from 8.5 cents declared a year ago.

(Reporting by Jasmeen Ara Shaikh, Nichiket Sunil ‚Äãand Keshav Singh Chundawat in ‚ÄåBengaluru; Editing by Alexander Smith, Edmund Klamann, Chris ‚ÄãReese and Eileen Soreng)

data.title: ANALYSIS-EU weighs two-track approach to break economic reform deadlock
data.description:
BRUSSELS, Feb 13 (Reuters) - EU governments have long preached unity as the best response to U.S. and Chinese economic pressure, but are discovering they need urgently to boost Europe's competitiveness and that waiting for all 27 member states to agree may take too long.

That was the clear message from an informal EU summit in Belgium on Thursday, where French President Emmanuel Macron and European Commission President ‚ÄãUrsula von der Leyen gave the bloc until June to progress on a stalled ‚Äåunion of financial markets.

The Capital Markets Union would allow the bloc to invest at scale at a level to match the United States, but vested interests in various countries and professional groups have held ‚ÅÝback any integration for more than a decade.

Now, unless there is sufficient progress by June, a smaller group of at least nine European Union ‚ÅÝmembers could press ahead in "enhanced cooperation" with the required steps and reforms, von der Leyen and Macron said.

"Often ‚Äåwe move forward with the speed ‚Äåof the slowest and the enhanced cooperation avoids that," von der Leyen told reporters on Thursday. "The¬Ýpressure and the sense of urgency is enormous, and that can move mountains."

The first movers could be ‚Äãthe six leading economies who took part in talks last month hosted by Germany ‚Äåon a "Europe of two speeds" to break decision-making inertia and galvanise the EU economy. The other five countries were France, Italy, Spain, Poland and the Netherlands.

If EU leaders needed a reminder of threats to their economic model, data showed on ‚ÄãFriday that the bloc's trade surplus shrank further in December as tariffs ‚Äãweighed on exports to ‚Äåthe U.S. and rising Chinese imports crowded out domestic production.

MULTI-SPEED EU ALREADY EXISTS

Some of those not invited to the German-led group of six have expressed alarm.

"It is a good idea to threaten member states to agree on some files, but a very bad idea ‚ÅÝfor the future of Europe," said one EU diplomat.

"Quite simply, it just flies in the face of the unity we like to ‚ÅÝparade in other areas," said another.

But a third diplomat said it was clear that plan A was to make progress with all 27 EU members and it was good to have a plan B.

"It's a hostile world out there. Prosperity is under threat if these bottlenecks continue,"¬Ýthe third diplomat said, noting that Europe already operated at different speeds elsewhere.

Certain flagship EU projects, including the euro currency and the Schengen passport-free travel zone, allow groups of EU countries to ‚ÅÝmove ahead, while ‚Äåothers can join later.

The Schengen area, for example, does not include Cyprus or Ireland, but does include four ‚Äånon-EU countries. The European Public Prosecutor's Office has 24 EU participants and the Unitary Patent system 18.

As recently as December, EU leaders agreed to provide a ‚ÅÝ90 billion euro loan to Ukraine, but the Czech Republic, Hungary and Slovakia opted¬Ýout.

Karel Lannoo, CEO of the Centre for European Policy Studies, said the EU should still try to advance as 27, not "become more concentric circles".

"You need to advance as Europe, and that's what we need to see also in the context of the threats from the United States and Russia," he said.

The European Policy Centre think-tank said a coalition of the willing was no silver bullet, noting that talks on creating the Capital Markets Union had been long held up by disagreements among big EU members, including France, Germany and Italy.

European Council President Antonio Costa, who chaired Thursday's meeting, said the ‚Äãtopic of a coalition of the willing was not part of the official discussions, but diplomats said it was raised on the sidelines.

"Europe has never really suffered from a lack of plans or grand vision; the real obstacle has almost always been weak implementation and national interests taking precedence over European ‚Äãones," ING Bank economists Carsten Brzeski and Bert Colijn said in ‚Äåa note to clients.

"Whether this time will be any¬Ýdifferent remains to be seen."

(Reporting by Philip Blenkinsop, Alex ‚ÄãChituc, Lili Bayer and Jan StrupzewskiEditing by Gareth Jones)

data.title: Biocon aims for revenue surge with rollout of generic weight-loss drugs
data.description:
By Rishika Sadam

Feb 16 (Reuters) - Biocon is aiming for high-double-digit percentage revenue growth as the Indian pharmaceutical firm prepares to launch generic versions of weight-loss drugs globally even as it remains ‚Äãcautious about an early rollout in the home market, ‚Äåa top company executive told Reuters.

The company is counting on demand for obesity medicines as it expands a pipeline ‚ÅÝthat includes copycat versions of Novo Nordisk's Wegovy, whose patent for semaglutide in ‚ÅÝa few markets expires in 2026.

Indian drugmakers, including Dr Reddy's, Lupin , ‚ÄåSun Pharmaceutical and ‚Äåat least half a dozen others are racing to bring cheaper copies to markets once the active compound goes ‚Äãoff patent.

Bengaluru-based Biocon is targeting a U.S. ‚Äålaunch of generic liraglutide in the first quarter of the next financial year, CEO Siddharth Mittal said in an interview on Friday. Liraglutide ‚Äãis also used for obesity treatment.

It aims ‚Äãto launch ‚Äågeneric Wegovy in Canada next year, subject to regulatory approval, Mittal said. It is also planning launches over the next few years in India, Brazil, Mexico, Turkey, ‚ÅÝand parts of the Middle East and Latin America.

The company, however, is ‚ÅÝcautious about an early start in India due to fierce price competition and local clinical trial requirements, Mittal said.

"There's going to be fierce competition in India," Mittal said, citing low price expectations. He said Biocon is exploring approval in a specific overseas market ‚ÅÝfirst, ‚Äåwhich could help it seek a clinical trial waiver in India ‚Äåunder local rules.

In India, Biocon would need to run a late-stage clinical trial before ‚ÅÝlaunch. The company is weighing whether that cost would be justified, Mittal said, or whether it should seek a waiver.

The obesity medicines market, according to several forecasts, is expected to reach at least $150 billion globally by the early 2030s, and analysts expect generic versions to be priced at least 60% below the originator products.

Biocon expects high double-digit percentage revenue growth, Mittal said. The company's ‚Äãannual revenue grew 5.4% in fiscal 2025 from a year ago, but it has been growing in early double-digits on a quarter-on-quarter basis.

India is not Biocon's main market. The ‚Äãcompany derives significant share of revenue ‚Äåfrom the United States and parts of Europe. (Reporting by Rishika ‚ÄãSadam; Editing by Tasim Zahid)

data.title: Nikkei dips as soft data, post-election lull cap moves
data.description:
TOKYO, Feb 16 (Reuters) - Japan's Nikkei share average drifted lower on Monday, with financials leading losses, as weaker-than-expected economic data and a ‚Äãpost-election lull capped moves.

The Nikkei 225 Index ‚Äåwas down 0.2% at 56,824.37, as of 0146 GMT. The broader ‚ÅÝTopix slid 0.7% to 3,792.63.

Preliminary October-December gross domestic ‚ÅÝproduct (GDP) figures showed that the Japanese ‚Äåeconomy missed economists' median ‚Äåforecast on softer capital spending, although it did reverse the previous ‚Äãquarter's contraction.

"I figured the ‚ÄåGDP figures would be treated as past figures, but seeing the Nikkei average struggling ‚Äãto gain, there may ‚Äãbe some slight ‚Äåimpact," said Masahiro Ichikawa, chief market strategist at Sumitomo Mitsui DS Asset Management.

The rally in Japanese ‚ÅÝequities following Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi's snap-election victory ‚ÅÝearlier this month may also have run its course for now, Ichikawa added.

The banking sector was down 3.22%, with Resona Holdings ( RSNHF ) and Mizuho Financial Group ( MFG ) ‚ÅÝdropping 7% ‚Äåand nearly 5%, respectively.

The largest loser ‚Äåby percentage in the Nikkei was medical equipment maker ‚ÅÝOlympus, down nearly 13%, following disappointing earnings. Printing and electronics firm Toppan Holdings fell 5.5%.

Bucking the overall sombre mood, shares of Sumitomo Pharma ( DNPUF ) surged 13.9% to lead the Nikkei's gainers by percentage after the drugmaker said Japan's health ministry ‚Äãwould review its iPS cell-derived therapy for advanced Parkinson's disease this week.

Credit card company Credit Saison ( CSASF ) jumped 9%, ‚Äãand furniture maker Nitori ( NCLTF ) ‚ÄåHoldings gained 8.2%.

(Reporting by Satoshi Sugiyama; ‚ÄãEditing by Sherry Jacob-Phillips)

data.title: NetActuate Enhances Mumbai Cloud Platform with ONE Capabilities
data.description:
Upgraded services in¬ÝMumbai, plus an existing Chennai PoP, strengthen options for performance, compliance, and connected architectures across India.

MUMBAI, India, Feb. 15, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- NetActuate, a leading provider of global infrastructure and network services in 45+ markets worldwide, today announced an enhancement to its cloud platform in Mumbai, India, enabled by the company's Open Network Edge (ONE) IaaS platform. NetActuate has upgraded and expanded cloud services at its Mumbai Data Center, including Public Cloud, Private Cloud, Virtual Private Cloud (VPC), and Hybrid Cloud offerings, to support growing demand in one of India's most important connectivity and technology hubs.

NetActuate has supported customers in India for more than a decade and also maintains a Data Center in Chennai. Together, they help customers improve regional reach, strengthen route diversity, and build resilient deployments for users across India, as well as for international connectivity and distributed application delivery.

The enhanced Mumbai services are delivered through NetActuate's Open Network Edge (ONE) Infrastructure-as-a-Service (IaaS) platform, built on open source software and standards, and managed through the customer portal. ONE provides everything needed to architect cloud environments and extensible infrastructure with out-of-the-box implementations of widely deployed operating systems, monitoring, and orchestration tooling, helping teams deploy faster and operate consistently across locations.

"NetActuate has had a presence in India for 10+ years and we've experienced continuous growth with our local and international partners," said Mark Mahle, CEO at NetActuate. The expansion ‚Äåwill also see Uber ( UBER ) enter Austria, ‚ÄãDenmark, Finland and Norway. ‚ÄåThe company expects the move to generate an additional $1 billion ‚Äãin gross ‚Äãbookings over ‚Äåthe next three years.

"We're seeing strong demand from merchants and consumers ‚ÅÝfor Uber's ( UBER ) platform - and we think ‚ÅÝit's time to raise the bar, shake things up and deliver better value across the ‚ÅÝcategory," ‚Äåsaid Uber's ( UBER ) global delivery head ‚ÄåSusan Anderson.

data.title: Short-dated Japanese bonds rally after economic growth data disappoints
data.description:
TOKYO, Feb 16 (Reuters) - Short-term Japanese government bonds (JGBs) rose on Monday following data that showed the economy grew far slower than expected last quarter.

The two-year JGB yield, the one most sensitive to ‚ÄãBank of Japan policy rates, decreased 1.5 ‚Äåbasis points (bps) to 1.265%. The five-year yield fell 1 bp to 1.670%. Yields move inversely to ‚ÅÝbond prices.

Japan's gross domestic product edged up 0.2% in the October-to-December quarter, ‚ÅÝofficial figures showed on Monday, short of the median ‚Äåestimate for a ‚Äå1.6% gain in a Reuters poll of economists. That followed a speech on Friday by ‚ÄãBOJ board member Naoki Tamura, who said ‚ÄåJapan is "very close" to achieving the central bank's 2% inflation target, signaling the chance of a rate increase in coming ‚Äãmonths.

"Board member Tamura may have deliberately ‚Äãused ‚Äåthe vague phrasing 'as the weather warms up' to leave room for a rate hike at the March meeting," Ataru Okumura, a senior rates ‚ÅÝstrategist at SMBC Nikko Securities, said in a client note.

The benchmark ‚ÅÝ10-year yield was flat at 2.210% after a three-day drop. The 20-year JGB yield climbed 1.5 bps to 3.060%.

Long-term JGB yields surged to record highs last month on concerns about the potential scale of stimulus ‚ÅÝfrom Prime Minister ‚ÄåSanae Takaichi, a fiscal dove.

But a measure of ‚Äåcalm has returned to the JGB market following a sweeping election victory ‚ÅÝfor Takaichi's party on February 8 on expectations the mandate will give her the leeway to keep to her pledge of "responsible" stimulus.

"It seems like the government of Japan is least cognizant of markets and what their impact might be," said Tom Garretson, senior portfolio strategist at RBC Wealth Management.

"If they don't push too ‚Äãfar on fiscal measures and eroding the balance sheet further, it looks like there's at least a balance between what the markets are willing ‚Äãto tolerate and the goals of ‚Äåthe administration," he added.

(Reporting by Rocky Swift in ‚ÄãTokyo; Editing by Thomas Derpinghaus)

data.title: US health regulators to consider safety status of processed ingredients, RFK Jr. says
data.description:
*

FDA to review status of processed food ingredients

*

Kennedy aims to close loophole pending White House approval

*

Kessler links ultraprocessed foods to chronic diseases

By Michelle Conlin

NEW YORK, Feb 15 (Reuters) - The Food and Drug Administration will consider a petition to revoke the safety status of dozens ‚Äãof processed refined carbohydrates unless food companies can prove they ‚Äåare safe and not contributing to health issues and obesity, U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. said ‚ÅÝin remarks that aired on Sunday.

He said the FDA would take up a request ‚ÅÝby former agency Commissioner David Kessler, who asked it last August to ‚Äåremove corn syrup and ‚Äådozens of other sweeteners and starches from the list of ingredients classified as GRAS, or Generally Recognized as Safe.

"We will act ‚Äãon David Kessler's petition," Kennedy told CBS' "60 Minutes" ‚Äåprogram. "And the questions that he's asking are questions that FDA should've been asking a long, long time ago." Kennedy and Kessler say the GRAS classification, enacted ‚Äãby Congress in 1958, has allowed the use ‚Äãof ingredients ‚Äåwithout a full government safety review because it lets food companies verify the safety of those items without oversight. Kennedy said that he intends to close that loophole if ‚ÅÝhe gets White House approval.

"There is no way for any American to know if ‚ÅÝa product is safe if it is ultraprocessed," Kennedy said on "60 Minutes."

Kessler, a pediatrician, was FDA commissioner from 1990 to 1997.

Two food industry trade groups, the Consumer Brands Association and the American Farm Bureau Federation, did not immediately respond to Reuters requests for comment. During his tenure heading the ‚ÅÝFDA, Kessler ‚Äåtried to regulate tobacco under the agency. The effort ultimately failed, but ‚Äåit helped put a greater spotlight on the tobacco industry.

He now wants the FDA to take ‚ÅÝthe same approach with large food companies.

"We changed how this country views tobacco," Kessler told the CBS program. "We need to change how this country views these ultraprocessed foods." Kennedy's campaign against processed foods and artificial dyes has been one of his most high-profile endeavors in office. The Trump administration last month announced new dietary guidelines that urge Americans to eat more protein and less sugar than previously advised, while avoiding highly processed foods.

But ‚Äãon Sunday's show, Kennedy stopped short of saying he would call for more government regulations.

"I'm not saying that we're going to regulate ultraprocessed food," he said. "Our job is to make sure ‚Äãthat everybody understands what they're getting, to have ‚Äåan informed public." (Reporting by Michelle Conlin in New York; Editing ‚Äãby Sergio Non and Alistair Bell)
